title: Number of 1 Bits
prompt:
Given a positive integer `n`, return the number of set bits (1s) in its binary representation.

This is also known as the Hamming weight of the number.

examples.output:
3
Explanation: The binary representation of 11 is 1011, which has three set bits.

1
Explanation: The binary representation of 128 is 10000000, which has one set bit.

30
Explanation: The binary representation of 2147483645 is 1111111111111111111111111111101, which has 30 set bits.

notesForAI.coreIdea: Use n & (n - 1) to clear the lowest set bit in each iteration, counting how many times until n becomes 0. Alternatively, check each bit with n & 1 and right-shift.
commonBugs: Using arithmetic right shift instead of logical right shift in languages with signed integers
Infinite loop if not using unsigned or logical shift for negative-looking bit patterns
Off-by-one from confusing 0-indexed bit positions with count
notesForAI.expectedComplexity: O(k) where k is the number of set bits
